6.dv: probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

16.gl: to procreate (properly, of the father, but by extension of the mother); figuratively, to regenerate
16.kj: bear, beget, be born, bring forth, conceive, be delivered of, gender, make, spring

18.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

20.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)
compare G34 (ἀγέλη)) (to bring tidings); a messenger; especially an "angel"; by implication, a pastor

22.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
22.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

23.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
23.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

24.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
compare G34 (ἀγέλη)) (to bring tidings); a messenger; especially an "angel"; by implication, a pastor
